work_unit: qq_push.py exception dict leak sanitize (M-003 + M-004)
situation: audit-2026-07-06-022 derived_fixes pending. M-003 (_get_token line 83 after H-005 edit) and M-004 (_upload_file line 133) both raise RuntimeError with full response dict {d} when access_token/file_uuid missing. QQ Bot token endpoint may return secret/appsecret; upload endpoint may return user openid or bearer tokens. Exception messages propagate to logs/cron mail/stderr — credential leak vector.
action: Sanitize both sites to expose only top-level keys via list(d.keys()) for field-name debugging without revealing values. Matches existing HTTPError redaction pattern at lines 79/115/129. Combined into single commit because same file, same remediation pattern, same audit source.

Reused existing qq-push worktree per route-fix reuse rule; cp-guard blocked opt-worktree.sh cmd_commit due to unmerged H-005 divergence → used case-104 stack-diverged escape hatch (direct wt edit + wt-internal commit), per memory [[Stack on diverged wt escape hatch]].

After M-005..M-017 all dispatched, consider merging opt-qq-push-1783344795 (now carries H-005 + M-003 + M-004) to clear cp-guard for any future qq_push.py fixes

audit_findings.finding: _get_token raises RuntimeError(f'无 access_token: {d}') exposing full token response dict including potential secret/appsecret fields
audit_findings.remediation: Replace {d} with keys={list(d.keys())} to preserve debuggability without leaking values

audit_findings.finding: _upload_file raises RuntimeError(f'上传无 file_uuid: {d}') exposing full upload response dict including potential user openid/tokens
audit_findings.remediation: Replace {d} with keys={list(d.keys())} to preserve debuggability without leaking values
